guardrails.title: No Server Code in Client Bundle
guardrails.body: Server-only code (database connections, secret references, etc.) shall not be included in the client bundle.

guardrails.title: Audit NEXT_PUBLIC_ Environment Variables
guardrails.body: NEXT_PUBLIC_ environment variables shall contain only values safe to expose to the client. Do not include secrets or internal URLs in NEXT_PUBLIC_ variables.

guardrails.title: Verify Against Official Docs
guardrails.body: Next.js APIs change frequently and AI knowledge may be outdated. Verify current API behavior against official documentation during implementation.

guardrails.title: Server Components by Default
guardrails.body: Use React Server Components as the default choice. Use the 'use client' directive only when hooks, event handlers, or browser APIs are required, and keep the scope of Client Components minimal.

guardrails.title: Route Handler vs Server Action Decision
guardrails.body: Use Server Actions for data mutations triggered from UI, and Route Handlers for API endpoints intended for external systems. Do not call your own app's Route Handlers from Server Components.

guardrails.title: Use Next.js Optimized Components
guardrails.body: Use next/image for images, next/font for fonts, next/link for internal links, and next/script for external scripts. Do not use HTML standard elements (img, link, a, script) directly. Import fonts only once in the root layout.

guardrails.title: Await Async Request APIs
guardrails.body: In Next.js 15 and later, cookies(), headers(), params, and searchParams are asynchronous APIs (Promises) and require await. Accessing them synchronously causes a runtime error.

guardrails.title: Suspense Boundary for useSearchParams
guardrails.body: Wrap components that use useSearchParams() in a Suspense boundary. Without this, the entire route falls back to client-side rendering.

guardrails.title: Parallel Route Slots Require default.tsx
guardrails.body: Each @slot in parallel routes must have a default.tsx. Without it, soft navigation results in a 404 error.

guardrails.title: error.tsx Must Be Client Component
guardrails.body: error.tsx files require the 'use client' directive. global-error.tsx must include html and body elements.

guardrails.title: No dynamic() with ssr:false in Server Components
guardrails.body: Specifying { ssr: false } with next/dynamic inside a Server Component has no effect. Use it only inside Client Components.

guardrails.title: No route.ts and page.tsx Coexistence
guardrails.body: Do not place both route.ts and page.tsx in the same route segment folder. Separate API endpoints and pages into different route segments.
